O_i=\{(X_{iv},Z_{iv},Y^{\rm obs}_{iv}):v\in\mathcal V\},\qquad
Y^{\rm obs}_{iv}=Y_{iv}(Z_{iv}),
\]
be observed. Retain iid replicated latent arrays, but generate \(Z_i\) from the known matched-block randomization law \(\nu\), independently of latent potential outcomes conditional on the recorded matching variables. Require \(\nu(gz)=\nu(z)\) under the declared within-block action; for pair matching, each pair has exactly one treated member and \(s_b\) swaps both member records and assignment coordinates. Latent complete records may appear in proofs and estimands only; every fitted predictor, selector, score, LP input, threshold, and reported interval must be measurable from observed data, known \(\nu\), prespecified target information, and auxiliary randomization.

2. Delete the three assumptions asserting external product-law, array-independent, and generic-independent draws of \(U_{i,a}\). They conceal the observability defect. Replace them by prespecified observable selector kernels
\[
\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\ge0,\quad
\sum_v\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=1,\quad
\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)=0\ \text{unless }z_v=a,
\]
equivariant under relabelling:
\(\kappa_{h,a}(\pi_gv\mid gz)=\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z)\).
Given observed \(Z_i\), draw \(J_{i,a}\sim\kappa_{w,a}(\cdot\mid Z_i)\), where \(\kappa_{w,a}=\sum_hw_h\kappa_{h,a}\), and set \(U_{i,a}=(J_{i,a},a)\). Then
\[
q^{\rm obs}_{h,a}(v,a)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{h,a}(v\mid z),\qquad
q^{\rm obs}_{w,a}=\sum_hw_hq^{\rm obs}_{h,a}.
\]

\[
S_{i,a}=\left|Y^{\rm obs}_{iJ_{i,a}}-\widehat m_a(X_{iJ_{i,a}})\right|.
\]
For the new target replicate, observe the target’s baseline \(X_{0T}\) but not use either \(Y_{0T}(a)\) in fitting or calibration, and define the proof-only target residual
\[
S_{0,a}=\left|Y_{0T}(a)-\widehat m_a(X_{0T})\right|.
\]
Keep one draw \(T\sim q_{\rm tar}\) and the two lifts \(T^0,T^1\); never replace them by independently drawn arm targets.

Preserve the stacked common-\(w\) LP theorem as the exact certificate for simultaneous unweighted exchangeability of both armwise score sequences.

\[
k=\left\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\right\rceil.
\]
If \(k\le M\), let \(r_{a,(k)}\) be the \(k\)-th order statistic of the \(M\) observable scores \(S_{1,a},\ldots,S_{M,a}\); if \(k=M+1\), set \(r_{a,(k)}=\infty\). Use the closed interval
\[
I_{a,w}=[\,\widehat m_a(X_{0T})-r_{a,(k)},\,
\widehat m_a(X_{0T})+r_{a,(k)}\,].
\]
Miscoverage is the strict event \(S_{0,a}>r_{a,(k)}\). Under exchangeability, break ties only for the proof by iid continuous auxiliary uniforms and lexicographic ranks; the reported interval retains weak inclusion and is therefore conservative under ties. No no-ties assumption and no unspecified “rank conditions” are permitted. Show
\(\Pr(S_{0,a}>r_{a,(k)})\le(M+1-k)/(M+1)\le\alpha/2\), then use Bonferroni—without arm independence—to obtain coverage of \(I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\) for the same \(T\).

For \(p_{a,O}=q_{{\rm tar},a}(O)\), \(q_{a,O}=q^{\rm obs}_{w,a}(O)\), impose the explicit, checkable overlap condition \(p_{a,O}>0\Rightarrow q_{a,O}>0\), and define \(\lambda_{a,O}=p_{a,O}/q_{a,O}\). For observed calibration orbits \(O_{i,a}\) and known target orbit \(O_{T,a}\), form the weighted empirical measure placing mass \(\lambda_{a,O_{i,a}}\) at \(S_{i,a}\) and mass \(\lambda_{a,O_{T,a}}\) at \(+\infty\), normalize by their total, and take its left-continuous \((1-\alpha/2)\)-quantile, with closed-set inclusion at equality.
